encounter.reason: Chronic heart-failure and atrial fibrillation maintenance follow-up
encounter.service_provider: University of New Mexico Hospital and Heart Failure Clinic
encounter.location: UNM Heart Failure Clinic, Albuquerque, New Mexico

labs.name: estimated glomerular filtration rate
labs.reference_range: greater than or equal to 60 mL/min/1.73 m2

labs.name: NT-proBNP
labs.reference_range: lab-specific adult reference threshold; above typical normal range

labs.name: glucose
labs.reference_range: lab-specific; above typical normal fasting range

conditions.name: chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, NYHA class II
conditions.what_changed: documented as clinically stable on guideline-directed medical therapy with stable home weights, mild exertional fatigue, trace ankle edema only, modest NT-proBNP elevation, and no medication escalation because renal function and potassium remain limiting

conditions.name: paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
conditions.what_changed: documented as controlled on anticoagulation; ECG showed sinus rhythm with occasional premature atrial complexes; symptoms limited to rare brief palpitations without syncope or known thromboembolism

conditions.name: chronic kidney disease stage 3b
conditions.what_changed: renal function reviewed with creatinine 1.67 mg/dL and eGFR about 35 mL/min/1.73 m2; kidney function explicitly cited as limiting further heart-failure therapy escalation

conditions.name: type 2 diabetes mellitus
conditions.encounter_role: known condition referenced in cardiology follow-up
conditions.what_changed: no diabetes regimen change by cardiology; glucose of 154 mg/dL noted and existing insulin glargine management deferred to other clinicians

conditions.name: hypertension
conditions.encounter_role: known condition referenced in problem context
conditions.what_changed: no separate assessment or medication change beyond routine blood pressure measurement showing controlled office blood pressure

medications.name: carvedilol 12.5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 12.5 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: sacubitril/valsartan 24/26 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 24/26 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: furosemide 40 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 40 mg by mouth every morning
medications.reason: chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction / volume management

medications.name: empagliflozin 10 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus and chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: apixaban 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 5 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: paroxysmal atrial fibrillation for stroke prevention

medications.name: atorvastatin 40 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 40 mg by mouth every night

medications.name: insulin glargine subcutaneous injection
medications.sig: continue current insulin glargine regimen managed by other clinicians

document_references.description: Longitudinal heart-failure clinic follow-up documenting stable chronic HFrEF NYHA class II and controlled paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. The note should summarize symptoms, exam, reviewed renal and cardiac labs, ECG findings, medication continuation without change, renal limitations on therapy escalation, and counseling on sodium restriction, daily weights, and return precautions.

Reinforcement of low-sodium diet, daily morning weight monitoring, medication adherence, and clear call/return precautions for weight gain greater than 3 lb in 24 hours, worsening dyspnea, increasing edema, chest pain, or persistent palpitations. Should also include follow-up timing of 6 months or sooner for symptom change.

diagnostic_reports.description: Hemoglobin value was reviewed, but there are not enough named CBC components in this encounter narrative to support a full report-with-results CBC manifest.
ECG interpretation documented normal sinus rhythm at 64 bpm with left ventricular hypertrophy and occasional premature atrial complexes; no separate structured waveform measurements were provided.
